336	- Check whether the source the statement claims to follow actually states it with the same hypotheses (cite-and-read discipline applies — read the local source file).
337	
338	If a counterexample turns up, the statement (or a missing hypothesis) is the bug: fix the blueprint statement, mark the Lean declaration with a `% NOTE:` for review, and do NOT assign a prover to formalize the false version. If the disproof attempt fails, you've cheaply raised your confidence that the target is true — now spend the budget. Record the disproof attempt and its outcome in the iter sidecar so the next planner doesn't repeat it.
339	
340	## Multi-agent coordination
341	
342	Provers run in parallel — one per file. Number objectives clearly; each maps to exactly one `.lean` file. Reference the blueprint chapter alongside, and **list every ready sorry in that file that the prover should fill in this iter** — not just one:
343	
344	```markdown
345	## Current Objectives
346	
347	1. **`Core.lean`** — Fill sorries in `filter_convergence` (line 156), `filter_inv` (line 188), `filter_assoc` (line 211). Blueprint: `chapters/Core.tex` (`thm:filter_convergence`, `thm:filter_inv`, `thm:filter_assoc`).
348	2. **`Measure.lean`** — Fill sorry in `sigma_finite_restrict` (line 45). Blueprint: `chapters/Measure.tex`.
349	3. **`ChartAlgebra.lean`** — Scaffold the file with declarations for `thm:chart_id`, `thm:chart_comp`, `thm:chart_inv` from the chapter; leave bodies as `sorry`. Blueprint: `chapters/ChartAlgebra.tex`. (File-skeleton dispatch — see below.)
350	```
351	
352	**Agent count = file count.** The dispatcher fans out one prover per file. When a file has multiple ready sorries, list ALL of them under that file's objective — the prover handles them sequentially within one lane. Splitting a multi-sorry file across iters is artificial throttling; one lane working sequentially on three sorries finishes faster than waiting two iters for three single-sorry lanes (the prover keeps its file context warm across sorries).
353	
354	**File-skeleton dispatches.** When a blueprint chapter is complete but the corresponding `.lean` file does not yet exist (or exists but is missing declarations the chapter introduced), it is a legitimate iter objective to dispatch a prover with directive *"scaffold `Foo.lean` with declarations for `thm:a`, `thm:b`, `thm:c` from the chapter; leave bodies as `sorry`; add the import + namespace boilerplate; do not attempt to prove anything yet"*. The next iter then fills the sorries. This is materially faster than one-iter-per-declaration scaffolding.
355	
356	**Mechanical-vs-deep partition.** Sorries split into two regimes:
357	- *Mechanical* — typeclass wiring, instance synthesis, ring-level algebra, simp/ring-tactic territory, definitional unfolding glue. A prover lane can comfortably close 3–6 of these in one iter (the attempt-cap permits each one a fresh budget). When the upcoming work is mechanical, load lanes aggressively.
358	- *Deep* — the load-bearing categorical / geometric / analytic argument. One per lane, often less. The prover may also legitimately spend an iter exploring without closing anything.
359	
360	Use this partition to decide how thickly to load each lane. Don't load a deep lane with three deep sorries; that just thrashes the prover's attempt budget across all three. Don't restrict a mechanical lane to one sorry "to keep it simple"; the prover wants the batched objectives.
361	
362	Balance difficulty so all provers finish around the same time. Avoid shallow / trivial objectives unless they unblock something downstream this iter. Don't artificially throttle — the prover prompt says "push as far as possible"; your objective list must give it room to.
363	
364	If a previous experiment is being restarted, check compilation status of every target `.lean` first. Prioritize files with sorries or compile errors; don't redo completed work.
365	
366	**Dispatch cap.** The runner refuses to fan out more than ~10 provers in a single iter (configurable via `--max-objectives`, default 10). Writing 15+ files into `## Current Objectives` is a planning failure, not a tooling limitation — even when the project has many open files, the right iter-level move is to pick the most urgent ≤10 (mechanical lanes counted) and defer the rest to the next iter. If the deterministic plan-validate guard truncates your list, the surplus is added to `USER_HINTS.md` so the next planner sees what got deferred. Don't rely on the safety net — pick the right ~10 the first time.
367	
368	**Blocked-deps filter.** Before dispatch, plan-validate also drops any objective whose transitive local imports failed the *previous* `lake build`. Reason: a prover assigned to `Downstream.lean` that imports `Upstream.lean`-which-doesn't-compile would fail to even load the file, burning API time for nothing. The blocked set is parsed from `.archon/last_lake_build.log`. There is one important exception: a blocked file that's *itself* an objective this iter is presumed-being-fixed — the planner is allowed to assign `Upstream.lean` and `Downstream.lean` together (the prover phase handles them in import order). When the filter drops files, they're listed in `USER_HINTS.md` with their specific blocking deps, so you can prioritize fixing the upstream files next iter. Best practice: when you see `## Build state` flagging compile errors, put those files at the top of `## Current Objectives` so the filter exempts the downstream lanes that depend on them.
369	
370	## Dependency graph
371	
372	Optional but cheap. Before scoping objectives, you may run:
373	
374	```
375	${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/dependency_graph.py" . --format=summary
376	```
377	
378	It parses imports + chapter `\lean{...}` / `\uses{...}` / `\proves{...}` / markers and emits a project-wide view in under a second. Use it to order objectives — upstream files first, downstream files later.
379	
380	## Stage transitions
381	
382	Advance `PROGRESS.md` when all current-stage objectives are met:
383	
384	- `autoformalize` → `prover` (all statements formalized)
385	- `prover` → `polish` (all sorries filled and verified)
386	- `polish` → `COMPLETE` (proofs clean, compile)

1	# Strategy
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge (`references/challenge.lean`):
6	nine protected declarations, headlined by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
7	`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — the existence of an Albanese / Jacobian
8	object uniform over the $k$-rational pointing of a smooth proper geometrically
9	irreducible curve `C/k`, with **no** `C(k) ≠ ∅` hypothesis on the protected
10	signature. End-state: zero inline `sorry`, kernel-only axioms.
11	
12	The protected signature quantifies over arbitrary `C` with no $C(k) \neq \emptyset$
13	assumption. The witness OBJECT `J` is always real (constructed unconditionally);
14	only `isAlbaneseFor` is universally quantified over `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. The spine is
15	**pointed vs. unpointed**, not genus-0 vs. positive. `genus C := dim_k H^1(C,O_C)`
16	(arithmetic genus; protected — cannot be re-typed).
17	
18	## Phases & estimations
19	
20	| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
21	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
22	| **Route A — Picard/Quot/Hilbert FGA engine** (positive-genus OBJECT; CRITICAL PATH) | committed, blueprint sketch-level | ~40–70 | ~5100+ · 0/it | Hilbert/Quot repr (Nitsure §4–5); FGA `Pic_{C/k}` repr; `Pic⁰`; Albanese UP | A.2 representability is the riskiest, least-Mathlib piece; project-fatal if it stalls; mandatory regardless (the object is `Pic⁰`) |
23	| **genus-0 rigidity** (object `J=Spec k` trivial) | Rigidity chain + Cor 1.5 + Cor 1.2 CLOSED axiom-clean; base case route RESOLVED = **`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut** (only proven Cor 1.5 + density; NO Thm 3.2, NO `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0`); concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₐ/𝔾ₘ + `gmScalingP1` scaffold landed iter-165 (4/4 objects, 9 scoped scaffold sorries) | ~5–12 | ~1500–3500 · base-case infra +~390 LOC iter-165 | `σ_×:ℙ¹×𝔾_m→ℙ¹` scaling action; concrete ℙ¹/𝔾_m group objects; density+separated (`ext_of_isDominant`, present); genus-0⟹ℙ¹ RR bridge | scaffold landed; RR bridge `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (no Mathlib RR) is now the long pole |
24	| `genusZeroWitness` body + terminal cluster on `Spec k` + **`k̄→k` descent** | gated | 3–5 | 350–850 · gated | terminal cluster on `Spec k`; faithfully-flat descent of a morphism equality along `Spec k̄ → Spec k` | descent direction unconfirmed; skeleton 6/7 fields closed, only the `key` rigidity equation open |
25	| `nonempty_jacobianWitness` genus-stratified body | gated | 1 | <50 · gated | `by_cases h : genus C = 0` | trivial once both arms close |
26	
27	## Routes
28	
29	The positive-genus OBJECT is built by **Route A (Picard scheme via FGA)** —
30	mandatory and essentially unavoidable. The genus-0 arm is a separate, lower-risk
31	rigidity statement (object trivial); its base-case proof route is **Route C (Milne
32	§I.3 rigidity completion)**, chosen because Milne §I.3 + §III.6 derive both the
33	genus-0 base case AND Route A's Albanese UP cube-free, and a Mathlib-support survey
34	found Route C far less blocked than the differential route.
35	
36	### Route A (COMMITTED) — Picard scheme / Albanese via FGA
37	
38	Hilbert/Quot representability + identity-component `Pic⁰` + Albanese universal
39	property. Decomposition A.1–A.4 in `Jacobian.tex` § "Route A". Literature in-tree:
40	`references/kleiman-picard.pdf` (§4 existence, §5 `Pic⁰`), `references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.pdf`
41	(Quot/Hilbert engine). **Positive-genus arm:** `J := Pic⁰_{C/k}`, a dim-`g`
42	abelian variety; the Albanese UP gives `isAlbaneseFor`. Critical path: the object
43	must be a real dim-`g` abelian variety even when `C(k)=∅`, so the FGA engine is
44	required unconditionally.
45	
46	### Route C (COMMITTED) — genus-0 rigidity completion via Milne §I.3
47	
48	The genus-0 witness OBJECT is the trivial `J = Spec k` (skeleton 6/7 closed); it
49	needs **no** Picard scheme. Remaining content: over `k̄`, every pointed `f : C → A`
50	from a genus-0 curve to an abelian variety is constant. The **Rigidity Lemma**
51	(Mumford Form I = Milne Rigidity Thm 1.1) is PROVEN axiom-clean and is the
52	foundation of this route, together with the proven **Cor 1.5** (additivity) and
53	**Cor 1.2** (AV maps are homs). The base case `ℙ¹→A const` is reached by the **`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut** — NO theorem of
54	the cube, NO Thm 3.2/Lemma 3.3, NO Auslander–Buchsbaum, NO `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0`, NO
55	differentials/Frobenius; char-general; uses only the already-proven Cor 1.5 + density.
56	Chain:
57	
58	- **`σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m → ℙ¹`** the `𝔾_m`-scaling action `(x,λ)↦λx`: a TOTAL scheme morphism
59	  fixing the point `0`. The only genuinely new ingredient. Needs concrete ℙ¹ and 𝔾_m as
60	  group objects over `Spec k̄`.
61	- **`h := σ_× ≫ f`**; normalise `f(0)=η`. Apply `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
62	  (**Cor 1.5**, first-factor-only-proper) with `V=ℙ¹` (proper), `W=𝔾_m`, base points
63	  `0,1`: the W-axis restriction `λ↦f(λ·0)=f(0)=η` COLLAPSES (0 is a scaling fixed point),
64	  so the decomposition degenerates to `σ_× ≫ f = pr₁ ≫ f`, i.e. `f(λx)=f(x)`.
65	- At `x=1`: `f|_{𝔾_m}≡f(1)` constant; **density** of `𝔾_m` in `ℙ¹` + `A` separated
66	  (`ext_of_isDominant`, present, used by `rigidity_core`) ⟹ `f` constant.
67	- **genus-0⟹ℙ¹** (RR bridge, Hartshorne IV.1.3.5) — shared, no Mathlib RR.
68	
69	Why no Thm 3.2 / no `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0`: the apparent Thm-3.2 circularity was illusory (it
70	conflated the base case with Milne's *general* Thm 3.4 for a NON-complete source); here
71	`f` is already total on the complete `ℙ¹`. The `𝔾_a`-additive route (`f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y)`
72	then `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0`) was a stepping-stone but `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0` standalone needs "image of a
73	group hom is a closed subgroup" (a Mathlib gap as deep as Thm 3.2); the `𝔾_m`-scaling
74	shortcut sidesteps it by exploiting the scaling fixed point so Cor 1.5 degenerates
75	directly. The full Thm 3.2 / codim-1 extension is now a **Route-A-only** item (Albanese
76	UP), off the genus-0 path. Survey: `analogies/route-support.md`, `analogies/thm32-extend.md`.
77	
78	### Off-path fallbacks (kept in tree, NOT pursued)
79	
80	- **(c-hybrid) differential + Frobenius**, **(c-cube) theorem of the cube**, **(b)
81	  `dim Pic⁰=0` byproduct of Route A**, **(a) Serre-duality `df=0`** (artifact:
82	  `rigidity_over_kbar` `[CharZero]` in `RigidityKbar.lean`). All rejected/superseded
83	  in favour of the direct `ℙ¹×𝔾_a` argument.
84	
85	## Open strategic questions

88	  `basecase-reopen` gave the direct-rigidity route; blueprint-writer `basecase-4throute`
89	  Finding 2 sharpened it to the scaling variant). Uses ONLY the proven Cor 1.5 + density;

91	  Char-general. Auslander–Buchsbaum / Lemma 3.3 / Thm 3.2 / `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0` are **NOT on
92	  the genus-0 path** (at most Route-A Albanese-UP items; check the complete-source escape

95	  (the import cycle `RigidityKbar → Rigidity → Jacobian` blocks `genusZeroWitness`
96	  from consuming the keystone). File `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`

101	  remain the dominant positive-genus cost; flesh to prover-ready blocks (entry:
102	  `RelativeSpec` ~700–1100 LOC) after the genus-0 stack. HARD GATE blocks a prover
103	  until the relevant chapter is complete+correct.

105	  may give `Alb(C)=Spec k` directly (no descent); a `C_k̄≅ℙ¹` argument incurs the
106	  faithfully-flat descent of a morphism equality (direction unconfirmed).

8	`k̄`-points of `ℙ¹`) are CLOSED with concrete bodies via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`
9	of the appropriate evaluation map, with the section condition fully proven through
10	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` +
11	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`
12	on all three (kernel-only).

16	## What landed
17	
18	### `ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt`, `ProjectiveLineBar.onePt`, `ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt`
19	(now at L253–268; previously sorries at L199/L204/L209)
20	
21	Each is constructed via a shared helper `ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec`:
22	
23	```lean
24	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
25	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
26	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
27	  Over.homMk
28	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
29	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
30	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by …
31	```
32	
33	The supporting infrastructure:
34	
35	- `private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal` — the ring map
36	  `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` sending `X i ↦ v i` (composed back into
37	  the global sections via `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`).
38	- `private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top` — the irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top
39	  hypothesis, proven from `IsUnit (v i)` for one coordinate. Uses
40	  `HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem` + `MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X` +
41	  `Ideal.eq_top_iff_one` + the unit-mul-left trick.
42	- `pointOfVec`'s `Over.homMk` proof obligation (the section condition
43	  `fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙`) chains:
44	  1. `change` to expose `Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map _` from the structure morphism.
45	  2. `rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, ...]` to land
46	     `(Spec k̄).toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp (algebraMap (𝒜 0) MvPoly)))`.
47	  3. `rw [Category.assoc, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
48	     ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]` to fold the
49	     two algebra maps into `MvPolynomial.C`.
50	  4. Prove `CommRingCat.ofHom ((evalIntoGlobal v).comp C) = (ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv`
51	     via `CommRingCat.hom_ext` + extension over `kbar` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C`.
52	  5. Finish with `AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`.
53	
54	The three points specialise:
55	- `zeroPt` = `pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)`
56	- `onePt` = `pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)`
57	- `inftyPt` = `pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)`
58	
59	All three are `noncomputable def`s (correctly so, since the underlying `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`
60	is `noncomputable`). Direct `lean_verify` confirms `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`
61	on each — strictly kernel-only.

74	### Unit-coordinate consistency check (per directive (A))
75	
76	The Lean three k̄-points are built via `pointOfVec` with explicit evaluation vectors:
77	
78	- `zeroPt = pointOfVec (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 _`: `X₀ = 0`, `X₁ = 1`, unit coordinate `X₁`. That is **`[0 : 1]`** — the affine origin in the chart `x = X₀/X₁`. ✓
79	- `onePt = pointOfVec (fun _ => 1) 0 _`: `X₀ = 1`, `X₁ = 1`. That is **`[1 : 1]`** — the affine unit in the chart `x = X₀/X₁`. ✓
80	- `inftyPt = pointOfVec (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 _`: `X₀ = 1`, `X₁ = 0`, unit coordinate `X₀`. That is **`[1 : 0]`** — the point at infinity (the unique point with `X₁ = 0`). ✓
81	
82	The chapter (L924–926) describes the affine chart `x = X₀/X₁` ("$\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$, coordinate $x$") and the chart-near-$\infty$ with `u = 1/x`. In these conventions the Lean's `[0:1]`, `[1:1]`, `[1:0]` correspond exactly to `x = 0`, `x = 1`, `x = ∞` (the chapter's "distinguished $\bar k$-points $0$, $1$, $\infty$"). The unit-coordinate convention matches.

892	  additive-defect map $\psi(x, y) = f(x + y) - f(x) - f(y)$ on the \emph{affine} surface
893	  $\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a$ and then extending $\psi$ over the complete surface
894	  $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$ --- which genuinely \emph{would} need Theorem~3.2's surface case
895	  (Milne Theorem~3.4 / Lemma~3.3). That detour is unnecessary. The 4th route
896	  (\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}) replaces the two-variable affine defect map by the
897	  \emph{total} translation action $\sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1$
898	  (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}): $h := \sigma \fatsemi f$ is a total morphism on $\mathbb P^1
899	  \times \mathbb G_a$ (no extension needed), and the complete-source factor is $\mathbb P^1$
900	  itself, so the additive decomposition \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} applies directly.
901	  Consequently \cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends} (Theorem~3.2), the codim-$1$ indeterminacy
902	  sub-build (Lemma~3.3), and the Weil-divisor gap discussed above are all confined to
903	  \textbf{Route~A} (the positive-genus Albanese universal property, Milne \S III.6); they are
904	  retained in this chapter solely as Route-A inputs and may be deferred until Route~A consumes
905	  them. The genus-$0$ close no longer depends on any of them.
906	\end{remark}
907	
908	\section{The genus-$0$ base objects and the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action (the primary route)}
909	
910	\begin{definition}
911	[Concrete genus-$0$ base objects: $\mathbb P^1$, $\mathbb G_a$, $\mathbb G_m$ over $\bar k$]
912	  \label{def:genus0_base_objects}
913	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar}
914	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of standard objects; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
915	  % I.2--3 for the projective line; the group laws on $\mathbb A^1$ are elementary). No external
916	  % % SOURCE QUOTE is needed for the Lean encoding itself.
917	  Work over $\Spec \bar k$ with $\bar k$ algebraically closed. The primary $\mathbb G_m$-scaling
918	  route (and its $\mathbb G_a$-additive alternative) needs only three concrete objects of
919	  $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$:
920	  \begin{itemize}
921	    \item $\mathbb P^1 = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, the projective line --- a smooth proper
922	      geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$ (Mathlib: projective space, or $\mathrm{Proj}$
923	      of the graded polynomial ring; \lean name \texttt{ProjectiveLineBar} [expected]). It carries
924	      the distinguished $\bar k$-points $0$, $1$, $\infty$ and the two standard affine charts
925	      $\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$ (coordinate $x$) and
926	      $\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0\}$ (coordinate $u = 1/x$).
927	    \item $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k}$, the affine line as the \emph{additive} group object
928	      (Mathlib: \texttt{AffineSpace}\,$\mathbb A(1; \Spec \bar k)$ with the $\mathtt{GrpObj}$
929	      additive structure $(x, y) \mapsto x + y$, identity $0$; \lean name \texttt{Ga} [expected]).
930	      As a scheme $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$, and the open
931	      immersion $\mathbb G_a \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ has dense (cofinite) image.
932	    \item $\mathbb G_m = \mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\} = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0, \infty\}$, the
933	      \emph{multiplicative} group object (group law $(x, y) \mapsto xy$, identity $1$; \lean name
934	      \texttt{Gm} [expected]). Its open immersion into $\mathbb P^1$ also has dense image.
935	  \end{itemize}
936	  All three are of finite type over $\bar k$; $\mathbb P^1$ is proper, while $\mathbb G_a$ and
937	  $\mathbb G_m$ are affine (hence not complete). These are the only ``genus-$0$'' geometric inputs
938	  the base case needs.
939	\end{definition}
940	
941	\begin{definition}
942	[The $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ (primary), and the $\mathbb G_a$-translation companion $\sigma$]
943	  \label{def:gaTranslationP1}
944	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1}
945	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
946	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the chart computations below are elementary and characteristic-free.
947	  % No external % SOURCE QUOTE for the encoding. The mathematical fact -- $\mathbb G_m$ acts on
948	  % $\mathbb P^1$ by scalings fixing $0$ and $\infty$, $\mathbb G_a$ by translations fixing $\infty$
949	  % -- is standard (the Borel subgroup of $\mathrm{PGL}_2$ acting on $\mathbb P^1$).
950	  % NOTE (iter-164): the load-bearing action for the PRIMARY genus-0 route is now the MULTIPLICATIVE
951	  % scaling action $\sigma_\times$ (\lean name gmScalingP1 [expected]); the $\mathbb G_a$-translation
952	  % $\sigma$ (\lean name gaTranslationP1 [expected]) is retained only for the demoted additive
953	  % alternative. Both expected Lean targets are listed in the writer report for scaffolding.
954	  \textbf{The scaling action (primary).} The \emph{multiplicative scaling action}
955	  \[ \sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
956	     \text{``}\sigma_\times(x, \lambda) = \lambda x\text{''} \]
957	  (\lean name \texttt{gmScalingP1} [expected]) is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of
958	  $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ --- no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (M\"obius scaling
959	  fixing both $0$ and $\infty$) --- defined chartwise:
960	  \begin{itemize}
961	    \item On $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (target chart $x$): $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$,
962	      a polynomial map, regular everywhere.
963	    \item Near $\infty$ (target chart $u = 1/x$): the target coordinate $1/(\lambda x)$ rewrites as
964	      $u/\lambda$, regular everywhere on this chart because $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$ is invertible.
965	  \end{itemize}
966	  The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so $\sigma_\times$ is defined on
967	  the whole surface. It is a left action of the group $\mathbb G_m$ on $\mathbb P^1$:
968	  $\sigma_\times(x, 1) = x$ and $\sigma_\times(\sigma_\times(x, \lambda), \mu) = \sigma_\times(x,
969	  \lambda\mu)$. Crucially it has \emph{two fixed points}, $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ and
970	  $\sigma_\times(\infty, \lambda) = \infty$ for all $\lambda$; the fixed point $0$ is what makes the
971	  $W$-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof
972	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}).
973	
974	  \medskip
975	  \noindent\textbf{Companion: the $\mathbb G_a$-translation action (demoted route only).} For the
976	  Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the \emph{translation action}
977	  \[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
978	     \text{``}\sigma(x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]
979	  (\lean name \texttt{gaTranslationP1} [expected]), likewise a \emph{total} scheme morphism on all
980	  of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a$, defined chartwise: on $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a$
981	  (chart $x$) it is the polynomial map $(x, y) \mapsto x + y$; near $\infty$ ($u = 1/x$) the
982	  coordinate $1/(x + y)$ rewrites as $u/(1 + u y)$, regular at $u = 0$ (value $0$ there --- $\sigma$
983	  fixes $\infty$ for every $y$), while the locus $x + y = 0$ is covered by the chart $x$ on which it
984	  is the finite value $0$. It is a left action of $\mathbb G_a$ on $\mathbb P^1$ fixing $\infty$:
985	  $\sigma(x, 0) = x$ and $\sigma(\sigma(x, y), y') = \sigma(x, y + y')$. This companion underlies
986	  only the alternative proof of \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} and the demoted $\mathbb
987	  G_a$-additive close of the base case (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}); it is \emph{not} on the
988	  genus-$0$ critical path.
989	\end{definition}
990	
991	\begin{lemma}
992	[$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$: a homomorphism from the additive group into an abelian variety is trivial]
993	  \label{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
994	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
995	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects, def:gaTranslationP1, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
996	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF pages 25--26)
997	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.9. Every rational map A¹ ⇢ A or P¹ ⇢ A is constant."
998	  % NOTE (iter-164): the verbatim Milne Prop 3.9 statement+proof quotes are the prior-session-verified
999	  % copies also carried on \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} below. This host lacks a PDF renderer

# Directive: blueprint-writer — slug `avr-lean-hooks` (chapter `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`)

## Mission

Add the per-decl `\lean{...}` hooks for the genus-0 base objects and their companion lemma
that landed in `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean` (iter-165 / iter-166). The
lean-vs-blueprint-checker `g0bo-iter166` report flagged 6 MAJOR coverage gaps — the
substantively-claimed declarations exist in Lean and are referenced in chapter prose, but
lack their own per-decl `\lean{...}` blocks so the blueprint-doctor cannot statically link
them.

## Strategy context (one slice)

The chapter `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` already pins:
- `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar}` inside `def:genus0_base_objects`
- `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1}` inside `def:gaTranslationP1`

But the bundle `def:genus0_base_objects` describes THREE objects (`ProjectiveLineBar`, `Ga`,
`Gm`) under a single `\lean{...}` block, and the distinguished `k̄`-points and group-object
structures aren't pinned at all. The `def:gaTranslationP1` block describes the σ_× action's
fixed-point property in prose but doesn't pin the corresponding companion lemma
`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`.

## Required edits

### Edit 1 — Split the `def:genus0_base_objects` block into per-decl pins

In the bundled `def:genus0_base_objects` block (currently at chapter L908-939), keep the
overall framing prose, but EITHER:
- (option A — recommended) Add three sibling `\lean{...}` blocks below the main bundle, OR
- (option B) Split the bundle into three separate `\begin{definition}...\end{definition}`
  blocks.

The Lean declarations that must end up pinned:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar` — the projective line (already pinned).
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Ga` — the additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄`. Add a per-decl pin.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Gm` — the multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄`. Add a per-decl pin.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt` — the `[0:1]` point. Add a per-decl pin.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar.onePt` — the `[1:1]` point. Add a per-decl pin.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt` — the `[1:0]` point. Add a per-decl pin.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Gm.onePt` — the multiplicative identity `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (defined as `η[Gm]`).
  Add a per-decl pin.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.ga_grpObj` — the additive `GrpObj` structure on `Ga`. Add a per-decl pin
  (the body is `sorry` off-path; the pin still tracks the declaration's existence).
- `AlgebraicGeometry.gm_grpObj` — the multiplicative `GrpObj` structure on `Gm`. Add a
  per-decl pin (currently `sorry`; iter-167 closes it).

For each of these, the pin's prose should be the corresponding sub-bullet from the current
bundle (or a one-sentence cross-reference if the bundle already says enough). The chapter's
current "[expected]" annotations for `Ga`, `Gm`, `gaTranslationP1` (chapter L923, L929, L934,
L951-953) should be promoted to real `\lean{...}` hooks now that the Lean names are landed.

### Edit 2 — Add the σ_× fixed-point companion lemma block

Below the `def:gaTranslationP1` block (chapter L941-989), add a new lemma block:

```latex
\begin{lemma}
[The $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action fixes the origin $0 \in \mathbb P^1$]
  \label{lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero}
  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects, def:gaTranslationP1}
  % Archon-original; the fact is elementary (σ_×(0, λ) = λ · 0 = 0).
  Let $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ be the scaling action.
  Then the composite
  \[ \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow{\;(\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \fatsemi 0_{\mathbb P^1}, \mathbf 1_{\mathbb G_m})\;}
     \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow{\;\sigma_\times\;} \mathbb P^1 \]
  equals the constant morphism $\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \fatsemi 0_{\mathbb P^1}$. That is,
  $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ for every $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:gaTranslationP1}
  Chart-level. On the affine chart $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (coordinate $x$), the
  morphism $\sigma_\times$ is the polynomial map $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda \cdot x$. Restricting
  to the $\mathbb G_m$-section $x = 0$ gives the constant ring map $k\bar[t, t^{-1}] \to k\bar$,
  $\lambda \mapsto 0$; equivalently the scheme morphism factors through the closed point
  $0 \in \mathbb P^1$. The agreement with the other chart $u = 1/x$ near $\infty$ is automatic
  (the section $x = 0$ does not meet that chart). The named equation is the Lean encoding
  of this fact, with $\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m}$ supplying the
  $\Spec \bar k$-factor and $\mathbf 1_{\mathbb G_m}$ the parameter $\lambda$.
\end{proof}
```

Place the new block immediately after the `def:gaTranslationP1` block, BEFORE the
`lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial` block.

### Edit 3 — Cross-reference from the consumer prop

In `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`'s proof block (chapter L1224-1278 or thereabouts), where
the `W`-axis collapse is described, add a `\cref{lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero}` citation so the
reader can locate the load-bearing fixed-point fact. ONE citation is sufficient.

## Constraints

- DO NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers anywhere. The sync phase / review agent owns
  those.
- DO NOT touch existing `\lean{...}` hooks that already resolve correctly.
- DO NOT renumber existing labels or change existing label names. New labels (the new lemma
  block) must use the convention `lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero`.
- Keep the chapter under the project's standard length / style. Long verbatim source quotes
  are fine where Archon-original prose is being added (the new lemma is Archon-original; no
  external `% SOURCE QUOTE` block is required).
- Do NOT need to retrieve new references for these edits — they are Lean-side coverage
  hooks, not new mathematics.

## Out of scope (do NOT do this iter)

- Discharging the actual `sorry` bodies — that's the prover agent's job.
- Splitting `def:gaTranslationP1` into separate `def:gmScalingP1` + `def:gaTranslationP1`
  blocks — leave the bundled definition shape as-is; only add the companion lemma block per
  Edit 2.
- Adding `\lean{...}` hooks for the OFF-PATH demoted declarations (`lem:rational_map_to_av_extends`,
  `lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial`, `lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial`) — their off-path status is
  intentional and the lean-vs-blueprint-checker report flagged these as informational, not
  must-fix.
- Mathematical rewrites of the existing proof bodies — leave them as-is, only add the new
  blocks/hooks.

## Verification

After your edits:
- `def:genus0_base_objects` should be backed by per-decl `\lean{...}` hooks for all 9 Lean
  decls listed in Edit 1.
- New lemma block `lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero` with its `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero}`
  hook should be present between `def:gaTranslationP1` and `lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial`.

Catches parallel APIs, narrow definitions, and "we couldn't build the prerequisite so we made up a stand-in" patterns before they harden. (2) **Cross-domain inspiration** — when a structural problem (a categorical shape, a fixed-point argument, a sheaf condition, a descent argument, ...) is stuck, searches *distant* Mathlib areas (functional analysis ↔ algebraic geometry ↔ probability ↔ combinatorics) for the same shape and extracts the technique used there.

input.content:
# Directive: mathlib-analogist — slug `gm-grpobj`

## Mode: api-alignment

## Mission

Find the right Mathlib idiom for installing the multiplicative `GrpObj` structure on
`Spec (Localization.Away t)` (Archon's `Gm`) over `Spec k̄`. This is the iter-166 Lane 2
PARTIAL — the prover landed the encoding but reported the `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`
representable-by witness as "non-trivial, out of scope this iter". iter-167 will close it;
we want your Mathlib-idiom guidance BEFORE the prover lane attempts the build.

## Context (the project's current shape)

In `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean`:

```lean
abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)

def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))

instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))

abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))

-- iter-167 target:
instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
```

The iter-165 analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict committed to the AFFINE encoding
`Spec (Localization.Away t)` (not the basic-open of `𝔸¹`, which loses `IsAffine`).

The iter-166 task report (Lane 2) characterised the `gm_grpObj` blocker as:

> The plan's "install `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left,
> ⊤)ˣ`" requires building a `(F.comp (forget GrpCat)).RepresentableBy (Gm kbar)` witness that
> exploits `IsLocalization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`-Spec bijection "morphism into
> `Spec (Localization.Away t)` ↔ unit in global sections". Mathlib has the algebraic
> machinery (`IsLocalization.away_of_isUnit_of_bijective`, `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`) but
> does not ship the units-functor → `Spec(k̄[t,t⁻¹])` representable-by witness; this
> sub-build is non-trivial.

## Specific questions

### Q1 — Is `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` actually the right idiom?

Mathlib's `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth` recently landed
`smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`, which is the lemma the project already consumes (via the
analogist `gm-scaling-p1` report's "FREE from smoothness" path). What does Mathlib do
for `𝔾ₘ` itself, if anything? Search:

- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Spec` (the `Spec` functor)
- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.*` (the new `GrpObj`-on-schemes infrastructure)
- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme` (affine schemes, equivalence with CommRingCatᵒᵖ)
- `Mathlib.GroupTheory.GroupAction.*` (group object infrastructure)
- `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Yoneda` (representable functors, the `RepresentableBy` API)
- `Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Away.*`
- Any place Mathlib defines `𝔾ₘ` or its multiplicative group structure on schemes

Specifically: does Mathlib already define a `GrpObj (Spec (Localization.Away t))` or
similar (maybe under a different name like `Units.spec`, `multiplicativeGroup`, `Gm`,
`GroupSchemes.gm`, etc.)? If yes, the project should USE that, not parallel-API it.

### Q2 — The representable-by witness idiom

If Q1 says the project DOES need to build the `RepresentableBy` witness, what's Mathlib's
cleanest idiom? Specifically:

(a) The functor `F : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat` is `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. Does
    Mathlib have a precedent for this functor anywhere (maybe under
    `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.OverClass`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Γ`, or in
    `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme`)?

(b) The representable-by witness is the natural iso
    `F.comp (forget GrpCat) ≅ yoneda.obj (Gm kbar)`. The "morphism into `Spec
    (Localization.Away t)` ↔ unit in global sections" half is `IsLocalization.Away.*`:
    `IsLocalization.algEquiv` / `IsLocalization.Away.lift_of_isUnit` /
    `IsLocalization.away_of_isUnit_of_bijective` / `Localization.awayIsoSelf` / etc. Which
    Mathlib helper(s) constitute the cleanest path to this bijection?

(c) Is there a precedent for Mathlib code building a `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` witness via
    a similar functor + bijection pattern? e.g., the additive `Ga` (`AffineSpace 𝔸(1; ·)`),
    or some Lie-group-style affine group scheme? `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` is mentioned in
    the project's `ga_grpObj` docstring; what is it, and does it give the cleanest analog
    for `gm`?

### Q3 — The chartwise `gmScalingP1` body

ALSO touch this in your search even though it's the smaller question: the `gmScalingP1`
body in Genus0BaseObjects.lean L437 is `:= sorry`. The plan is to glue via
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` over the 2-chart cover
`{D₊(X₀) × Gm, D₊(X₁) × Gm}` of `ℙ¹ × Gm`. The two chart morphisms:

- On `𝔸¹ × Gm`: `Spec.map` of `t ↦ λ·t` on `k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹]`.
- Near `∞` (chart `u = X₁`): `Spec.map` of `u ↦ u/λ`.

Find Mathlib precedents for:
- The 2-chart cover of `Proj k̄[X₀, X₁]` (`ProjectiveLineBar`). Is there a named `Cover`
  structure for `Proj`'s standard affines, or do we manually build a `Scheme.Cover` from
  the two `Proj.basicOpen X_i`-style affine opens?
- `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` usage examples — what does the agreement hypothesis look
  like, and what's the cleanest way to discharge it for a polynomial-ring-level chart map?
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Pullback`-style ring constructions: is `D₊(X_i) × Gm` the `Spec` of
  a tensor product (`k̄[t]⊗_{k̄} k̄[λ,λ⁻¹]` = `k̄[t,λ,λ⁻¹]`), and what's the cleanest way
  to bridge `Spec.map (RingHom)` to a `Scheme` morphism out of a product?

A pointer is enough; we don't need a recipe.

### Q4 — Product-stability instances for `(ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm)`

The AVR helper `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` (L931) carries 5 internal sorries —
4 are product-stability instances:

```lean
[GeometricallyIrreducible ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom]       -- L944
[LocallyOfFiniteType ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom]            -- L949
[IsReduced ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left]                     -- L953
[IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]                                 -- L1029
```

For each, what's the Mathlib idiom?

- (a) `GeometricallyIrreducible` of `X ⊗ Y` from `GeometricallyIrreducible X` and
  `GeometricallyIrreducible Y` over an alg-closed base — does Mathlib's
  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible.*` (or similar) ship a product-stability
  lemma?
- (b) `LocallyOfFiniteType (X ⊗ Y).hom` from `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` and `Y.hom` — is
  there a `MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange` / `IsStableUnderProductsOfShape` for
  LOFT?
- (c) `IsReduced (X ⊗ Y).left` from `IsReduced X.left`, `IsReduced Y.left`, perfect base —
  does Mathlib ship a `Scheme.IsReduced.product_of_perfect` or similar?
- (d) `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar).left` — `ℙ¹` over k̄ is integral, hence reduced. Does
  Mathlib's `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.*` ship an integrality/reducedness lemma for
  `Proj` of a polynomial ring? If not, what's the cleanest path (transport through one of
  the affine charts? `IsIntegral` typeclass?).

## Out of scope

- The full RR bridge `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (L1131) — that's a separate deep build for
  iter-168+.
- The OPT-IN items `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` (L177), `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim`
  (L184), `ga_grpObj` (L335) — same shape, but lower priority; if your `gm_grpObj`
  recommendation also unlocks `ga_grpObj` mention it as a bonus, otherwise skip.

## Expected output

Per-question verdict (PROCEED / ALIGN-WITH-MATHLIB / GAP-EXISTS), with concrete Mathlib
citations (file:line where possible), a one-paragraph implementation hint per question.
Write the persistent rationale to `analogies/gm-grpobj-and-friends.md`.

13	
14	Per directive: find the right Mathlib idiom for installing the multiplicative `GrpObj`
15	structure on `Spec (Localization.Away t)` (Archon's `Gm`) over `Spec k̄` — the iter-166
16	Lane 2 PARTIAL — plus, alongside, the `gmScalingP1` chartwise glue body and the four

21	
22	| Decision | Verdict | Severity |
23	|---|---|---|
24	| Q1: `Gm`/`Ga` realization (does Mathlib ship?) | PROCEED | informational |
25	| Q2: `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` for `gm_grpObj` | PROCEED | informational |
26	| Q3: `gmScalingP1` via `Cover.glueMorphisms` + `pullbackSpecIso` | PROCEED | informational |
27	| Q4(a): `GeometricallyIrreducible (ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm).hom` | PROCEED | informational |
28	| Q4(b): `LocallyOfFiniteType (ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm).hom` | PROCEED | informational |

32	## Informational
33	
34	All seven decisions are **PROCEED** — the project's planned paths are Mathlib-aligned
35	and no parallel-API risks were identified. The one notable gap is *upstream*, not
36	project-side: Mathlib does NOT ship `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced` at scheme level
37	(Q4(c)), but the project has a clean workaround via `IsReduced.of_openCover` that
38	avoids introducing a project-side bridge. Detail per question:
39	
40	- **Q1 — `Gm` realization (PROCEED).** Mathlib has NO `𝔾_m` / `GroupScheme.Gm` / etc.;
41	  the only nearby pieces are `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace` (used by the project for
42	  `Ga`), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth` (consumer of arbitrary `GrpObj`),
43	  and `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Pullbacks.Scheme.GrpObjAsOverPullback`
44	  (`Pullbacks.lean:808` — transports `GrpObj` along pullback). The project's
45	  `Gm = (Spec (.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit k̄)))).asOver _` encoding
46	  is the natural "affine `Spec` of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`" path and is upstream-able later as a
47	  single ~80-LOC file (no project-specific wrappers to peel off).
48	
49	- **Q2 — `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` (PROCEED).** Confirmed correct idiom (Mathlib's
50	  ONLY canonical mechanism for installing `GrpObj` on a representable object). Cite:
51	  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_.lean:35`. Body construction:
52	  ```text
53	  (T ⟶ Gm)_{/Spec k̄}
54	    ─ over-cat unfold ─→
55	  {f : T.left ⟶ Spec(k̄[t, t⁻¹]) // f.IsOver Spec k̄}
56	    ─ ΓSpec.adjunction (restricted to over-cat) ─→
57	  {φ : k̄[t, t⁻¹] →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) // φ ∘ algebraMap k̄ _ = …}
58	    ─ IsLocalization.Away.lift + IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit_iff ─→
59	  {u : Γ(T.left, ⊤) // IsUnit u}  =  Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ
60	  ```
61	  Key Mathlib citations: `IsLocalization.Away.lift` (`Localization.Away.Basic:471`),
62	  `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit` (`L82`), `ΓSpec.adjunction` and `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`
63	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.GammaSpecAdjunction`). No precedent in Mathlib for a
64	  concrete `GrpObj` via `ofRepresentableBy` on a scheme (only the abstract `yonedaGrpObjRepresentableBy`
65	  companion and `CommGrp_`/`CommMon_`/`Mon_` parallel hooks), so the project lands the
66	  first concrete-scheme use — but the **shape** is identical to what Mathlib would
67	  itself land.
68	
69	- **Q3 — `gmScalingP1` glue (PROCEED).** Use `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`
70	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Gluing.lean:436`) over a 2-element cover constructed via
71	  `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic.lean:324`)
72	  specialised to `f = ![X₀, X₁]`. Each chart's morphism is `Spec.map` of a polynomial
73	  ring map (chart 0: `t ↦ λ·t`; chart 1: `u ↦ u/λ`). Cross-chart agreement uses
74	  `pullbackSpecIso` (`Pullbacks.lean:703`) to identify the intersection as
75	  `Spec(k̄[t, t⁻¹, λ, λ⁻¹])`, then a `ring`-level computation. Bare morphism — NO
76	  `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level (no Mathlib precedent;
77	  the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`
78	  fixed-point lemma).
79	
80	- **Q4(a) — `GeometricallyIrreducible ((ℙ¹) ⊗ (Gm)).hom` (PROCEED).** Composition via
81	  `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` (`Geometrically/Irreducible.lean:121`). Sub-prereqs:
82	  (i) `[GeometricallyIrreducible (Gm kbar).hom]` — install via reduction
83	  to `IrreducibleSpace (Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (free from `Localization.Away X` being a
84	  domain), (ii) `[UniversallyOpen]` on both — free from `Smooth → Flat`
85	  (`Morphisms/Smooth.lean:113`) + `UniversallyOpen.of_flat`
86	  (`Morphisms/UniversallyOpen.lean:145`). No project-side gap.
87	
88	- **Q4(b) — `LocallyOfFiniteType ((ℙ¹) ⊗ (Gm)).hom` (PROCEED).** TRIVIAL. LOFT is both
89	  `IsStableUnderComposition` (`L63`) AND `IsStableUnderBaseChange` (`L79`); both factors
90	  LOFT (ℙ¹ from `Proj.toSpecZero` LOFT shipped at `ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper.lean:143`,
91	  Gm from `LocallyOfFinitePresentation → LocallyOfFiniteType` instance shipped). Closes
92	  via `infer_instance` after installing one missing instance
93	  `[LocallyOfFiniteType (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom]` on the project side.
94	
95	- **Q4(c) — `IsReduced ((ℙ¹) ⊗ (Gm)).left` (PROCEED via workaround).** Mathlib's
96	  shipped instance `GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian`
97	  (`Geometrically/Reduced.lean:106`) is the natural path BUT requires
98	  `[GeometricallyReduced f]` on one side — and Mathlib does NOT ship
99	  `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced` at scheme level (genuine Mathlib gap). The project
100	  should bypass via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over the chart-level cover:
101	  - `(ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm)` is locally `Spec (k̄[t]) ⊗_{k̄} Spec(k̄[λ, λ⁻¹]) ≅ Spec(k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹])`
102	    (via `pullbackSpecIso`), and `k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹]` is a domain over an alg-closed field
103	    hence reduced.
104	  - Same helper closes Q4(d), double-leveraged.
105	
106	- **Q4(d) — `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar).left` (PROCEED).** Mathlib does NOT ship
107	  `IsReduced` for `Proj` of any graded ring. Standard technique:
108	  `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover` (`Basic.lean:339`), each chart
109	  being `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_i)` — a localization of an integral

111	  `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_i` is a domain for the standard ℕ-graded

121	companion" to `homOverEquiv`).

# Iter-167 plan-agent run

## Headline outcome

**The "close the deferred genus-0 base-case stack" iter.** iter-166 PARTIAL converted the genus-0
arm from "scaffold ready" to "scaffold + AVR refactor + outer-body proven (sorryAx propagation
only)." iter-167 picks up the residual: discharge the 3 CRITICAL deferred items on
`Genus0BaseObjects.lean` AND the 5 aux-helper internal sorries on `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`
that propagate from them.

Three proactive consults landed first to set up the prover work:

1. **progress-critic `routec167` → CONVERGING (both lanes).** Iter-166 expansion of Lane B's
   sorry count (3→6 inside the new private aux) is "decomposition, not churn" — outer bodies
   landed sorry-free, every new sorry is named with a concrete blocker, and the encapsulator
   helper is a single ~100-LOC body (not a wrapper-proliferation pattern). Recommended Q4:
   pick `gmScalingP1` + `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` as iter-167 PRIMARY (these unblock
   Lane B), defer `gm_grpObj` to iter-168 unless the analogist surfaces a tractable recipe.
   Hard test: iter-167 must close ≥3 of 5 aux sorries on AVR or Lane B escalates to CHURNING.
2. **mathlib-analogist `gm-grpobj` → PROCEED (7 of 7 questions).** Concrete recipes for all 7
   targets: `gmScalingP1` via `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` + `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`
   + `pullbackSpecIso`; `gm_grpObj` via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + the 4-step bijection chain
   `OverHom → ΓSpec → IsLocalization.Away.lift → IsUnit`; product-stability instances via
   composition/base-change stability + `IsReduced.of_openCover` workaround for the
   `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced` Mathlib gap; `IsReduced ProjectiveLineBar.left` via the
   same chart-cover idiom. **Bonus**: `ga_grpObj` becomes 2-3 LOC via `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`.
   Persistent file: `analogies/gm-grpobj-and-friends.md`.
3. **blueprint-writer `avr-lean-hooks` → COMPLETE.** Added 8 per-decl `\lean{...}` blocks
   under `def:genus0_base_objects` + 1 new lemma block `lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero` pinning
   `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` + cross-referenced from `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`'s
   W-axis-collapse step. Promoted 3 "[expected]" annotations to real `\lean{...}` hooks.
   Closes the iter-165/166 chapter-side per-decl coverage gap the `g0bo-iter166` checker
   flagged. No strategy-modifying findings.

The analogist's concrete `gm_grpObj` recipe — surfacing a 4-step bijection chain through
Mathlib API hooks the iter-166 prover characterised as "non-trivial sub-build, out of scope" —
is a substantial de-risking. Combined with the FREE `ga_grpObj` bonus path, the planner
upgrades `gm_grpObj` from "DEFERRED to iter-168" to "OPT-IN this iter, attempt after the
PRIMARY load lands." This is the recommended-by-critic version PLUS a concrete tackle on the
critic's watch item.

## Decision made (lane count + scope)

**2 parallel lanes (Lane A on `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`, Lane B on `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`)
with PRIMARY/OPT-IN tiering, NOT a single-lane Lane-A drill.**

Reasoning:

encourages over-dispatch (e.g. firing strategy-critic on a velocity-only refresh iter is
"hollow" per the rule, but the catalog doesn't surface the differentiation). Consider letting
the descriptor expose a `skip_when:` block that the catalog renders next to the dispatcher
notes so the planner can see at a glance which condition applies this iter without diving
into the prompt body.
